title: plectranthus kassneri
intro: Plectranthus kassneri, also known as 'Mountain balm', is a flowering plant species in the Lamiaceae family, native to South Africa. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, primarily due to its essential oils that contain compounds such as borneol and camphor, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Plectranthus kassneri include relief from respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its use in treating wounds and skin conditions. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used as an infusion, tincture, or essential oil, and is sometimes combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
